blocks.content: Not because she was done — the painting wasn't done — but because there was something she seemed to need to say while she was standing still, before the evening moved too far into ordinary things and she lost the nerve.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I need to tell you something.
She said it carefully. Not like she was performing a speech — like she was deciding, right now, whether to say the next sentence.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I've been careful. About this. About people. Not the way most people are careful — not for the reasons you'd expect. Different ones.
He waited. He was good at waiting.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: There's something about me. Something I'm not ready to explain yet. And I need you to know it's not because I don't trust you.
She paused. The apartment was very quiet.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I'm just — still figuring out how to say it to someone who—
The word landed in the room and stayed there.
